title: Letter Case Permutation
description: Given a string s, you can change the case of each letter in s. Return all possible permutations of s that result from changing the case of the letters. The output should be in any order.

examples.explanation: For the input 'a1b2', we can change the letters 'a' and 'b' to either lowercase or uppercase. This results in 4 combinations: 'a1b2', 'a1B2', 'A1b2', and 'A1B2'.
In the input '3z4', the letter 'z' can only be changed to uppercase, leading to 2 combinations: '3z4' and '3Z4'.
The input '12345' does not contain any letters, hence the only permutation is itself: '12345'.

constraints: The input string s will have a length of at most 12.
The string s will only contain alphanumeric characters.
hints: Consider using backtracking to explore all combinations.
You can use recursion to build the permutations character by character.
Think about how to handle digits, since they do not change case.
Using a list to collect results can be helpful to manage the permutations generated.
